zh.lifeSummary: Isabel Jermyn是John Jermyn和Joan Ryton的女兒，於1435年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可朗 斯特拉頓。她於1458年與Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire在英國英格蘭諾福爾可登斯頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1494年在英國英格蘭享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabel Jermyn是John Jermyn和Joan Ryton的女儿，于1435年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可朗 斯特拉頓。她于1458年与Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire在英国英格兰諾福爾可登斯頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1494年在英国英格兰享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: Jermyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   German   or   Jeremy  .

variants.name: German
meanings: English: ethnic name from Old French germain ‘German’ (from Latin Germanus). This sometimes denoted an actual immigrant from Germany, but was also used to refer to a person who had trade or other connections with German-speaking lands. The Latin word Germanus is of obscure and disputed origin; the most plausible of the etymologies that have been proposed is that the people were originally known as the ‘spear-men’, with ancient Germanic gēr, gār ‘spear’ as the first element.

Spanish and Hungarian (Germán); English (of Norman origin) and German: from the personal name Spanish and Hungarian Germán, Old French Germain, German German, from Latin Germanus (see Germain ), a Christian saint's name.
Americanized form of German   Germann   1, a name of ancient Germanic origin.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: Isabel Jermyn was born in 1435, in Long Stratton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of John Jermyn and Joan Ryton. She married Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire in 1458, in Dunston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1494, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabel Jermyn est née en 1435, à Long Stratton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de John Jermyn et Joan Ryton. Elle a épousé Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire en 1458, à Dunston, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1494, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Isabel Jermyn wurde 1435 in Long Stratton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von John Jermyn und Joan Ryton geboren. Sie hat 1458 in Dunston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1494 im Alter von 59 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Jermynは1435年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクロング ストラットオンでJohn JermynとJoan Rytonの娘として生まれた。1458年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクダンストンでSir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquireと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1494年にイギリスイギリスで59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Jermyn(은)는 1435년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 론그 스트래턴에서 John Jermyn(와)과 Joan Ryton의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1458년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 던스턴에서 Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1494년 영국 잉글랜드에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel Jermyn родилась в 1435 г. в местности лонг страттон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали John Jermyn и Joan Ryton. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire в 1458 г. в местности дунстон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1494 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 59 лет.
